notes: The set coheres as one study rather than a slideshow. The thread (Psalm 22:18's two acts - divide the garments and cast lots) is established visually in the hook (#1), grounded in its author (#2 David, #3 a death not his own), proven (#4 the written line, #5 the seamless coat, #6 the fulfilment at the cross, #9 the stripped garments), deepened (#8 the wider execution written early, #11 the thousand-year bridge), and landed on the gospel pivot (#7 cross hero -> #10 the plan -> #13 his name -> #14 laying down his life). Tone is consistent (bleak dusk and lamplit shadow warming only at the close), Christ is uniformly 'passion' variant and consistently described, and the proof never drifts onto the contested 'pierced' reading. One advisory, non-structural note carried from the panels: six Christ-on-cross scenes and several scroll appearances should be kept visually distinct at render so the recurrence reads as deliberate motif, not repetition - this is a rendering caution, not a planning conflict.
